name: Max Strength
description: Low-rep (1-5RM) compound loading with barbell or kettlebell targeting maximum force production and neuromuscular recruitment.

name: Strength Endurance
description: Higher-rep work under significant load — circuits, complexes, and density intervals that develop the capacity to sustain force output over time.

name: Relative Strength
description: Strength expressed as a ratio of bodyweight — gymnastics, calisthenics, ring work, and weighted bodyweight movements demanding high skill alongside force production.

name: Aerobic Base
description: Zone 1-2 steady-state work — running, rowing, cycling, hiking, or swimming at conversational pace — building the aerobic engine and fat oxidation capacity.

name: Anaerobic Intervals
description: Short, maximal-effort intervals at Zone 4-5 — Tabata, 400m sprints, 30-sec all-out pieces — targeting VO2max and lactic capacity.

name: Mixed Modal Conditioning
description: Multiple movement types combined under time pressure — AMRAPs, chippers, GPP circuits, and barbell/KB complexes that tax systemic conditioning broadly.

name: Power / Explosiveness
description: Fast force production — Olympic lifts, kettlebell ballistics, plyometrics, and depth jumps where rate of force development is the primary training target.

name: Mobility / Tissue Prep
description: Joint health, range of motion development, and tissue preparation — loaded stretches, joint circles, fascial work, and active mobility drills.

name: Movement / Skill
description: Locomotion, coordination, pattern acquisition, and movement quality — tumbling, balance, crawling, flow sequences, and technical skill practice at sub-maximal intensity.

name: Durability / Work Capacity
description: Loaded carries, rucking, sandbag work, and GPP circuits that build the capacity for sustained output under fatigue — the foundation of operational and expedition fitness.

name: Combat Sport
description: BJJ drilling, positional sparring, grip conditioning, and isometric endurance work specific to grappling sports — mat time that has no substitute.

name: Rehab
description: Injury-specific therapeutic loading — isometrics, low-load progressive exercises, joint prep, and clinical protocols applied under injury or post-operative constraints.
